author: Ludwig1770
title: Look elsewhere
content:
Scanning albums on a NAS just doesn’t work in the real world - it only works if you scan ~5 albums at a time.  Any screen lock activity cancels the scan.  Changing to a different screen in the same app cancels the scan.  The developer’s solution is to use the desktop app.  Which doesn’t exist on Windows.
It seems odd that there just doesn’t seem to be any consideration of the user experience.

author: Amis2021
title: Excellent app for private music library
content:
Old fashioned idea now days perhaps, but I believe in OWNING my own music. I have been looking for a good app to play and organise my music. This does it all and well in my opinion. Love the option to select higher sample rates for the higher quality tracks. Equaliser is great with a range of presets. Plenty of customisation of screen layout. Love the customisable Audio Player — select an album switch to audio player and easily access the controls required. 

I’ve been using it for a month now every day and it has been working seamlessly on my iPad. 

One complaint: I can’t create nested playlists. I would love to be able to create categories: Christmas, jazz, pop, etc.

author: Petersiva
title: No iTunes support
content: Wanted an app I could use to play my iTunes playlists using the equaliser. Paid a premium but no support for iTunes. Furthermore it states nowhere in the FAQs about this!!

author: georgecnc
title: Developer should give you a week ad free.
content: Trying this app out with a serious view to buying it. It does a lot and there are a lot of ways of doing things so you have to spend time to learn it. But then there’s the Ads every 2 minutes. Very annoying. And really putting me off buying it. The developer should  give new users a week or two ad free so you can learn its functionality and  don’t lose interest through ad avoidance.

author: ConorTutt
title: Fantastic!!
content:
I have a lot of my own mixes that I listen to in work and have done for years. This app makes it super convenient for me to do so. I absolutely LOVE the amount of personalisation you can make to tailor the app and display specifically for you. 

Only other thing i’d love is a swipe right or left to jump forward / backwards 10 etc second and perhaps a waveform option at the bottom?

Other than that.. PERFECT.

author: BillTyros
title: Great APP
content: I have tried several other Apps, including the Music app built in my IPAD, but this one is far better. Even the free version is more than adequate. I have upgraded to the Premium Version though, simply to show a but of support for the developers. Hope they continue to develop the App and get the support from users that they deserve.

author: Asterlation
title: Pretty much perfect
content: This is a well designed and robust music player. I use it to stream from my NAS to Apple Car Play. It is deep.

author: Giljoc
title: Metadata fails
content: Paid £30 for the lifetime sub,  have a large music library.  Metadata reading got stuck at 2004/11634 fails to read any the remaining.  App advises use to pc software to read meta for large libraries.  No windows software available.  £30 wasted.

author: Magnus H, Oxfordshire UK
title: Awesome music player
content: Does what I need it to, and very well.

author: Matagaal
title: Best I could find do iOS
content: Simple and with great sound. Adding reverb would be great

author: James56346744688
title: A truly irritating app
content: Hassle upon first install then an immediate paywall so I couldn’t test it out.

author: Rockafella213
title: Amazing app
content: The app is amazing the way it has all my albums sorted and all that but I have one problem in the music Library the songs duplicate which messes up the albums because the albums become 2x longer

author: fiddins
title: Fantastic for remote library
content:
Works very nicely indeed with my library held in dropbox, the new last.fm addition is also greatly appreciated. 

It does take a long time to synchronise and update the media library without much useful updates to the user is my only complaint, but once it is set up its great.

author: Larkrise38
title: Great App
content:
Only had Flacbox for a few weeks now, but I’m really pleased with its features and ease of use.

I uploaded over 5,000 songs from my NAS drive to a new Playlist and it was faultless.

If you are looking for an excellent Music Player this is the one for you.

author: dhdietbfifnshdusksb
title: Keeps closing while I’m listening to music. Useless.
content: I also still got pop ups after paying.

author: whooshbiff
title: An excellent app
content: So good I subscribed🙂

author: Bilco-free
title: Stopped working
content: No longer plays my files

author: enkaytee
title: Excellent music player
content: I've been searching for 'the' music player to set me free from Apple Music and I'm pretty sure I've found it. Flacbox is a real game changer for me - the sound quality is great - specially with high res files, the interface is intuitive and well featured and transferring files from my desktop is easy. It's also got the ability to stream direct from my Mac which is a big bonus....👍

author: max123356
title: Ruzzian Supporters
content: Deleted  after noticed ruzzian services supported by this up.

author: jmsgrime
title: Constantly skips
content: Plays the first few seconds of a song, then skips forward 2 or 3 songs, plays one in full, starts skipping again.

author: Ebi Patani
title: Dev Replied to feedback
content: Thanks and I’m still learning as I am!

author: kazzaplayspinball
title: just test this out, it’s the best
content:
loving flacbox, tested it out for free and it’s SO GOOD, i paid up. Am streaming to my amplifier for amazing playback of high quality digital music through my ol’ freestanding gale 3030s. Happy happy.

It even reads my saved itunes library of low grade mp3s with no problems.

(ipad pro IOS 17.6 ish 2021)

author: codevolunt
title: Flacbox for iOS
content: I needed an app to stream .flac files from my Dropbox account. Still early days, but the limited version has worked well, without the restrictions that many developers impose making it difficult to decide if it is worth paying for. In this case, it probably is.

author: cynicalr
title: Pretty Good Flac Player
content: Nice clean interface.  Good features.  Getting tags from DLNA does not work, but support came back quickly with a good workaround and importing via smb or Dropbox works fine.  Very reasonable price too !

author: Na2ru1
title: Keeps losing connection with Mega
content:
Seems to be useless for a while with Mega cloud connection. I removed Mega and attempted to log back into it. No longer connects. Just an endless load sign on login page. 

Dropbox always worked fine but that isn’t my main cloud storage

author: TheJahanman
title: No more Fit for Purpose
content: Premium and buying the App out is not enough, now pay up to get off their Ads Still on premium BTW , blatant thiefs , bye

author: StephenBD
title: All round good
content: Well designed interface. Reliable SMB connection to the music files on my laptop. I’m using it to output hi-res FLAC files via my iPad to a Marantz streamer via a USB cable and it works every time.

author: Jane&goldador
title: Great App  - I use it to learn my choir songs
content: Great App  - I use it to learn my choir songs

author: Girl-Can-Mac
title: Below Average. Not great value.
content:
The sound quality could be better, considering it’s paid for.
The equalizer function leaves a lot to be desired.
The only thing I like about this mp3 player is my music, which struggles to sound good on this player.

author: Wetdive
title: Good player
content: I have paid for the pro version and started to download albums. However some have failed for no apparent reason. I have contacted support so hopefully they can resolve the issue very quickly if not then I will be requesting a refund. I don’t mind paying for something that works but begrudge paying for something that is useless

author: Mad Scientist57
title: Unintuitive
content:
Unintuitive, cluttered and fiddly user interface with poor to nonexistent documentation.

I need a music player to do two things:

Connect to my music collection 

Play my music.

That’s it!

I couldn’t even figure out the first step. Gave up and deleted the app.

author: MrColinWilson
title: Too fiddly
content:
I purchased the IOS and Mac versions of this app hoping to free myself from Apple Music and play my digital music files on my devices without expensive subscriptions.
The IOS app is better than the Mac version but in the end I gave up and deleted them both.
The interface is ugly and fiddly to set up (more so on the Mac) 
It does play FLAC files well, assuming you can sync them with the app, which I found to be a hit or miss.

author: Gregor_J
title: Annoying having to reorder files once added
content: Its a great player but I wish it would add files in numbered order or add folders as albums.

author: hd rixnen
title: Data collection
content: There is no need for this app to collect any data. Anonymous or not, data collection is stalking. Would purchase premium but only if app is “No Data Collected”

author: L@ur£n
title: Awesome!
content:
I bought songs from the Energy106 store online and they are digital downloads.
This is the best app to run all my mp3s on.
Tried other apps but they where only playing one song selected. 
This app allows your songs to be played from one song to the next without it stoping. 
Runs smoothly.
Excellent

author: Rare hare
title: Difficult
content: Hard to set up

author: eethom
title: Excellent product
content:
nice functional product
would be nice to be have a randomise function in the playlist sort option
Currently there is only the manual option which is useless for large playlists

author: Mah gid dont donload
title: OMG.. BLOWN AWAY
content: This is such a good app if you would like to add all your favourite song to one playlist!! I know currently, the rating is 4.6, but honestly I think it’s a teeny bit underrated :)!

author: Vindictor
title: Just missing SSH
content:
Great music player. It can play Flac, and many other formats, from your Google Drive, OneDrive, and various other cloud storage providers. We just had another update, and I got excited for a moment…. Because the only feature I’m REALLY waiting for is the ability to connect to a music library via SSH / SFTP. Then my entire home music library will be available on my iPhone and iPad, no matter where I am, without having to first upload it anywhere, or pay for extra cloud storage that I don’t need as I have plenty of storage at home, on the end of a fibre connection. Having said that, I do also wish the music player would display the codec of the current song, it would make it easier if you have both AAC and Flac copies of albums in a library. Would also be cool if it would show a “24 bit” or “HD Audio” icon when playing back hires files. of course this should be toggleable for those who don’t want to see this info, but I’d leave it on all the time. Small niggles. The player looks great, works great… sounds great! This app has the only software EQ that I’ve ever thought actually enhances the sound. Hoping the next update will provide SSH (I know a number of people who’re waiting for this) and display FLAC or AAC or MP3 on screen during playback. Thanks for allowing me to enjoy my flac collection without having to convert everything…. At least while I’m at home (for now) via SMB. I’ll be dancing in the streets when I can stream my music, outside, via SSH.

EDIT: I was just looking at installing Nextcloud on my server. Sadly I don’t see this in the list of supported cloud servers, either? It’s just that having TBs of storage at home, it’s a waste to pay for extra cloud services from which to stream music.  So I’m still hoping SSH (or maybe next cloud) can be supported, soon.  Hell, if they could implement SSH/SFTP file browsing/streaming then they could even branch out and allow remote photo viewing, and make a full remote media viewer / player.

author:  Customer_952
title: I want to move in together with this app!
content:
A fantastic app. The coverage of file types is, I believe, unsurpassed by any other app on the store!

As another reviewer says, Win*o#s files play on it as well. This is a huge bonus as I have thousands of WMA Lossless files from my peasant days on that OS (BICAM - Before I Could Afford Macs)!

There are still things the developers need to do, I’m quite sure they have a cunning plan, & hopefully they’ll implement it soon and make this THE BEST app on the store - by a Mile/Kilometer/ Trillions

author: Leonxxx96
title: Downloaded as an alternative to VLC. Much Better
content:
Nothing massive to complain about- but I do have suggestions that would make the experience much more pleasing.

-make the interface more customisable such as colour (themes), maybe allow for a colour palette that users can change rather than the default themes...
-this is picky but I feel like the artworks rounded corners are a bit excessive- maybe reduce the round ess or even make that feature customisable
- I think the audio player is amazing but one thing that bugs me is the buttons surrounding it are all saturated and greyed out- I think it would be much better if the button had a higher contrast to the background (darker). 
-the smoothness when you swipe the audio player down is slightly laggy, please make this transition faster.
-another thing is the startup wall where it gives you the app name and icon when you first open the app- I don't mind that feature existing but try and make it faster as when your in a rush it can be frustrating (I speak from experience)

author: Mr. Relentless Survivor
title: Best Music Player
content: This app is amazing; it plays so many file types.  I have so much music on my Windows PC that I could not play on my iPad Pro using the standard iTunes.  I ran across this app and it plays all my music, even Windows Media files.  I’m pleasantly surprised.

author: SpGrel
title: Very flakey. How if I get my money back???
content: Paid for the full version to sync from my NAS. Playback is hit and miss. Doesn’t continue to next track. Album artwork is wrong. Generously a poor experience. Stick to the free version or look elsewhere. I’m really annoyed I paid for this rubbish.

author: Ch6984
title: Could be 5 star
content: By far the best looking cloud music player I’ve come across but the loading time between tracks is way too long. If you could implement something like CloudBeats where the start of the next track is pre buffered and switches instantly, this would be the best there is.

author: ben0070
title: Works however album art isn’t loading properly
content: Please fix, will update to 5.

author: Steve Hodges
title: Too bad.
content: Very sad that this was the only cloud player I could find that could download in offline mode, Google Drive Flacs and play them in hi-res. Unfortunately it is not usable for me as the tracks start a second in when I skip. The ordering of tracks are not correct and I can’t seem to play them in standard sequence order. The rest is fine but these bugs make it unusable for me which is really sad. Happy to pay for these kind of bug fixes. iPhone 13 Pro.

author: Reynulds
title: Playlist m3u
content: Great App however the app does not recognize already created playlist, not even in the same folder with the audio files. I have created playlist for all my files and wish the App could recognize them instead of having to create over hundreds of playlists over again

author: John_in_Herts
title: Doesn’t play FLAC from a NAS
content:
Doesn’t play FLAC from a NAS.

Buy an alternative

author: changderson
title: For those missing control of their music
content: I’m in the UK and to buy the full app (one time purchase not subscription !!!!) is less than a tenner. If you spend that the app does most you can think of and more. I haven’t used it much but today someone sent me an mp3 and I was glad to have a place to save the mp3 (in files>>Flacbox). I hope to sort out my library soon :)

author: genghoe
title: Flac player
content:
First time try and found this app was amazing.sound is incredible clear and clean when playing my hi res audio.
Eq was a extra booster for the music.
Thanks

author: Gogobop
title: FlacBox
content: This app is great for iPhone users if they have a laptop/PC and lots of music to transfer using the WI-FI transfer option as so far it’s been flawless. The equaliser can accommodate many custom settings that are quick to access for different headphones and/or genre’s of music. There is plenty of settings and options in general that make this app shine brighter than anything else I have tried over the years for iPhone and I couldn’t be happier finding it. One thing that I would like though to make it perfect for me is to be able to have a loudness level setting to level out all songs to a similar ( preferably same ) volume to save having to raise and lover the volume. This is an issue because of the loudness wars plus different sources of master volume settings across all music and not the fault of FlacBox, it would just be great to have a way of getting around this issue that drives many people crazy. I purchased this app because I want to support the developers and would be happy to purchase more extra features that would take this app to the far and beyond. Thank you FLacBox Creators ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

author: Porrohman_
title: Frustrating but promising?
content:
Shows much promise but fails drastically for me as using as an SMB front end. I’ve only found this and Music Streamer that are in the area I want, both have inadequacies.

1. Scan time is just out of this world ridiculous, it’s like it uses carrier pigeons. MS takes about 20 minutes to scan the folder that this presumably would take several days, I haven’t had the patience to find out yet.

2. My Flac/MP3 collection includes all of the artwork in the folders or embedded. This app ignores that and goes to the internet to find the wrong images 30-40% of the time. There is no way of updating the image yourself so you are supposed to just put up with an ambient album that has a picture of a rap artist... MS deals with this almost perfectly, using the folder images and has the ability to copy/paste an image when needed.

3. It doesn’t seem to pick up the album/artist tags correctly all of the time, but maybe it’s because I haven’t had a scan complete.

Outside of these major problems, I really like the look of it and the play behaviour which certainly wins over MS which also has it’s own bizarre usability choices. I will keep an eye on this in the hope it will be improved, but in the meantime MS wins my loyalty. Wrong artwork is far far far worse than none at all...

author: shortofapicnic
title: I like it but...
content:
It still crashes pretty frequently. 
But the absolutely, bone-crushingly, life-sappingly worst thing about this app is when you want to add songs from your NAS to be stored on your phone. If you have a large library and want to add a song by, say, Yazoo or ZZ Top, you have to scroll through the hundreds of artistes who come before in the alphabetical list. There’s no quick way to jump to a particular letter as there is in iTunes. And then - this makes me want to cry with frustration - the app jumps back to the music list. You can’t stay within the Yazoo folder and choose songs from a second album - no - you have to go through the whole scrolling rigmarole again. Aaarghhhh! Sort it out, guys.

author: Meon123
title: Versatile Flacbox
content: Have been using Flacbox for about 2 months and it seems stable for my requirements that includes Hi Res.  Sound quality is good on ALAC and FLAC files.  I also use the Onkyo Hi Fi player and Qobuz for streaming and purchasing music.  Flacbox has very useful features including easily importing files from cloud storage and a graphic equaliser that works on the digital source to my Chord Mojo; Flacbox is the only App that can do this. I like to slightly adjust the sound frequencies to suit my tastes and hearing.  Ive compared sound quality with the Onkyo and I think there is slight clipping of high frequency clarity with Flacbox (equaliser off) but the ability to adjust the bass on Flacbox is brilliant.

author: Robthefluter
title: Some issues
content: Most of my ripped CD collection is from windows media player and is in WMA format. Latterly I did some in Flac. This and VLC are the only players I have found other than Windows Groove which will handle both formats as well as work directly from my OneDrive cloud store. Groove is no longer available for iOS and I want a replacement. The scan speed and artwork issues noted by other reviews are bad but not necessarily showstoppers. I have also noticed that the sound quality from WMA files is inferior to that produced by the VLC player on the same files. With Flac files there is less difference so I think there is a faulty WMA codec in use here. At least this app will attempt to put albums together from the cloud files. VLC Doesn’t do that and seems unacceptably basic in operation.

author: mcewan71
title: My go-to player
content: Pleasantly surprised with this app. I had tried a couple of higher res players and this is the best in terms of sound quality. But the cherry on top is the cloud integration: works seamlessly with my OneDrive. Also - when there were difficulties with this recently, I contacted them to highlight this. Not only did they come back to me promptly to explain they were working on an upgrade, but they also contacted me by email the following week to let me know the update and fix were ready. Kudos!

author: DAMI HENNI
title: Nice
content: Been using this for a while now connected into the cars Bluetooth system.  Sound Quality is very good & I can easily upload my own tracks from my drive which it allows you to connect also,  10/10 great work 😁 tried loads off apps but stuck with this one. In future maybe I’d like more sound options like a surround and 3D sound feature or things like reverb then this will be the top app
